title: Power-gate domains with isolation and retention
description: Shut off a domain only after adding isolation cells on its outputs and retention on state that must survive.
body: Use when a block can be fully powered down during idle periods. Do not power-gate a domain whose outputs feed live logic without isolation, because floating outputs propagate unknowns. Actions: place isolation cells to clamp outputs during power-down, add retention to registers that must keep state, and verify the power-up sequence restores operation. Validation signals include power-aware simulation showing clean isolation and correct state restore after wake. Risk boundary: a missing isolation cell corrupts the always-on side, so treat any ungated domain crossing as a blocking defect.
